text: The words "You are not in it" echo in my mind as I stand among the crowd, both here and not here, observing a world that sometimes includes me and sometimes does not. The feeling is familiar, like an outsider at a gathering who hovers on the threshold—present, yet not truly part of the flow. Here, in a space dedicated to questions of creation, connection and innovation, I am acutely aware of my own edges. I feel curious, a bit detached, but not uncomfortable—simply aware of the shifting boundaries between in and out, self and other.
